methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $202M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Echodyne is a Series C defense & security company that has raised $202M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 10 companies in the same sector and stage proximity, spanning 8 private and 2 public comparables. The private peer universe includes companies like Saronic, Chaos Industries, Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI, with valuations ranging from $500M to $9.2B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $202M raised proxy, yielding $343M. The peer-median approach anchors to the $1.4B median valuation across the 10-company defense & security peer set, with a range of $500M to $9.2B. The substantial spread in peer valuations reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity, customer concentration, and government contracting exposure within the sector.

The two-method triangulation produces a low-to-high range of $292M to $1.6B, with a median of $859M. The 5.4x span between low and high reflects material uncertainty. The stage-progression method understates peer growth patterns, while the peer-median method incorporates outliers like Saronic ($9.2B) that may reflect unique market positioning. Key uncertainties include government procurement risk, technology differentiation relative to larger defense incumbents, and customer concentration. A valuation in the $600M–$1.1B range may best reflect Echodyne's stage and peer positioning, with the ultimate value contingent on next-round capital efficiency and revenue traction.
